title: Alaska’s unprecedented food stamp backlog is taking a harsh toll on rural communities

title: Failing at polls, election deniers focus on state GOP posts

summary: After voting for a resolution to condemn the “horrors of socialism” and voting against removing Rep. Ilhan Omar, a Minnesota Democrat, from the Foreign Affairs Committee, Peltola expressed frustration with the state of affairs on Capitol Hill.

title: Chinese balloon high over US stirs unease down below
summary: The Chinese balloon drifting high above the U.S. and first revealed over Montana has created a buzz down below among residents — and raised a chorus of alarm from elected officials.

title: Former Dunleavy budget hawk Donna Arduin returns to work in the Alaska Capitol
summary: Arduin was Gov. Mike Dunleavy’s first and most controversial budget director, but she was ousted in 2019. Now she is back as a legislative aide, helping to craft a fiscal plan.

title: 2 arrested in California shooting that left 6 dead, including infant
summary: Two gang members were arrested early Friday, one after a gunbattle, in the January massacre of six people at a home associated with a rival gang, the Tulare County sheriff said.

title: ADN Politics podcast: Is this the end of the Pebble mine?
summary: In this episode, host Elizabeth Harball discusses what’s ahead for the mine and the Bristol Bay salmon fishery with ADN reporter Alex DeMarban.

title: Texas power woes linger as New England girds for deep freeze
summary: Rising temperatures offered some hope Friday for frustrated Texans days after they lost power — and in many cases heat — in a deadly, icy storm.

title: Newly released audio shows Trump’s Wisconsin campaign staff knew he lost election but continued to ‘fan the flame’
summary: The political operatives in the strategy session praised Democratic turnout efforts in the state’s largest counties and appeared to joke about their efforts to engage Black voters.

title: Alaska’s Republican former House speaker joins Democrat-led minority
summary: Kodiak Rep. Louise Stutes had asked to join the Republican-dominated majority, but she was rebuffed.

title: Anchorage students put their literary know-how to the test in annual Battle of the Books
summary: Teams of three and four from around the district participated in the annual competition, which quizzes students’ knowledge on a select group of books.

title: Skull found 25 years ago in Interior Alaska identified as man who likely died in bear mauling
summary: Investigators used genetic genealogy to tentatively identify the deceased as a New York man, Gary Frank Sotherden, who officials believe may have died as the result of a bear mauling in the 1970s, troopers said.

title: College Board faces storm of criticism over AP African American studies
summary: The College Board and several professors who worked on the Advanced Placement course said the removal of various lessons and authors was all strictly a matter of pedagogy, not politics. Others saw darker motives.

title: Republican-led House votes to oust Democrat Ilhan Omar from major committee
summary: House Speaker Kevin McCarthy was able to solidify party support against the Somali-born Muslim woman in the new Congress although some GOP lawmakers had expressed reservations.

title: Alaska State Troopers release body camera policy for public review as APD rollout remains in limbo
summary: The state Department of Public Safety plans to test the devices in Mat-Su and the Interior and on the Kenai Peninsula this spring. In the meantime, the Anchorage Police Department has no set date for putting body cameras into action despite growing public pressure to do so.

title: Public safety pension bill makes it through first hurdle in Alaska House, with many to come
summary: A measure to create a new defined benefits pension plan for public safety employees cleared its first of four House committees on Thursday, in a telling step for the legislation’s fate in a divided chamber.

title: Trump declines to say if he’ll support eventual 2024 GOP presidential nominee
summary: Former President Donald Trump is refusing to say whether he’ll commit to backing the 2024 GOP presidential candidate if it’s not him, injecting uncertainty into Republican hopes of reclaiming the White House next year.

title: Frustration grows in Mat-Su as school bus driver strike continues; union says negotiations to resume
summary: Bus drivers packed a school board meeting Wednesday evening, a day after they dropped students at school and then walked off the job, citing safety and pay concerns.

title: Frustration with power outages mounts in Texas capital as icy storm lingers
summary: Across Texas, 430,000 customers lacked power Thursday, according to PowerOutage.us. But the failures were most widespread in Austin.

title: In a rare move, U.S. releases ‘high-value’ Guantanamo prisoner
summary: Majid Khan was captured in 2003 and taken to secret CIA facilities, where he was subject to harsh interrogation tactics before being sent to Guantanamo.

title: Spending time in nature may protect against the risk of dementia
summary: Older adults who lived in an area with more green space had a lower rate of hospitalization for some diseases and dementias, a large study showed.

title: CDC issues alert on eye drops linked to bacteria that sickened dozens of people and killed one
summary: Disease investigators have linked the infections, including some found in blood, urine and lungs, to EzriCare Artificial Tears.

title: Dear Annie: Struggling to rebuild family after ugly divorce
summary: Dear Annie: Struggling to rebuild family after ugly divorce

title: 5.0 earthquake shakes Southcentral Alaska
summary: An earthquake with a magnitude of 5.0 struck west of Cook Inlet just before 6 a.m. and was widely felt in the Anchorage area.

title: Hunter Biden’s lawyers, in newly aggressive strategy, target his critics
summary: Hunter Biden’s attorneys urged prosecutors to investigate Rudy Giuliani, Steve Bannon and others for abusing personal information from Hunter Biden’s laptop.

title: A simple weeknight dish, this kale tomato rice with broiled cheese is pure comfort
summary: This dish is also a great way to sneak in extra vegetables like spinach, sliced carrot, zucchini or mushroom.

title: Emotional farewell for Tyre Nichols in Memphis marked by calls for justice
summary: Days after the horrific footage of Tyre Nichols’s beating by Memphis police was released, the city gathered for a funeral marked by anguished calls for justice.

title: As White House advances Willow oil project, advocates uneasy about Biden administration’s ‘mixed messages’
summary: The Interior Department released a final environmental review for the project Wednesday, and a decision is expected as early as March.

title: Ask Elaine: Should I change directions after being let go from my job?
summary: A reader tries to figure out what’s next after being let go from their job.

title: McCarthy sees possible ‘common ground’ after meeting with Biden on debt limit
summary: Speaking to reporters outside the White House after the meeting, McCarthy said he and the president agreed to address the debt limit in a “responsible” way and that they are not discussing cutting Social Security or Medicare.

title: Gas stoves are back under scrutiny with new US limits proposed
summary: An Energy Department proposal would set limits on energy consumption for gas stoves, drawing concerns that the regulation could end the use of some products.

title: Amazon, Apple spent more on lobbying last year than ever before
summary: In total, the top tech companies spent nearly $70 million on lobbying in 2022, outstripping other industries including pharmaceuticals and oil and gas.

title: College Board revises AP Black history class after criticism from conservatives
summary: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, a possible Republican presidential candidate in 2024, said he was blocking the course because it pushes a political agenda.

title: Alaska labor commissioner resigns for undisclosed reasons
summary: Tamika Ledbetter unexpectedly resigned Tuesday from her position as head of the Alaska Department of Labor and Workforce Development, causing Gov. Mike Dunleavy to name Deputy Commissioner Cathy Muñoz the agency’s acting boss.

title: Giant Willow oil project on Alaska’s North Slope nears final approval
summary: Biden administration officials are preparing a key environmental assessment to say the Interior Department can grant partial approval to the ConocoPhillips project.

title: Tyre Nichols beating raises scrutiny on ‘elite’ police units
summary: Police departments across the U.S. deploy plainclothes squads of officers in unmarked cars to tackle increasing crime rates or get guns off the street. But these groups often employ aggressive tactics, use force more often than their uniformed counterparts and lack supervision.

title: Patient who brought in weapon fatally shot 2 men at Delta Junction rehab facility, charges say
summary: Daniel Serkov, 33, is accused of creating an elaborate plan to conceal the shooting.

title: Alaska’s top-funded earmark is a $99M gym at Fort Wainwright. Officials say it will promote military mental health.
summary: The fitness annex will house an indoor track and AstroTurf field where soldiers can train and build community during winter months.

title: As the Colorado River dries up, states can’t agree on saving water
summary: California did not sign on to a joint proposal for cuts - an impasse that suggests the wrangling over how to conserve the dwindling water supply that serves 40 million people will continue in coming months.

title: Police reform talks are back in Congress, but there’s little hope for a deal
summary: Republicans now control the House, and they’ve shown little interest so far in the type of policing measures discussed in 2021.
